rule.name: Gin Debug Mode Enabled
rule.rule_desc: Detects debug mode settings in Go web frameworks.
rule.vuln_desc: Debug mode can expose internals and verbose diagnostics.
rule.reviewer: Verify framework mode defaults in production runtime. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Gin TLS Validation Bypass
rule.rule_desc: Detects TLS certificate verification bypass in HTTP/TLS clients.
rule.vuln_desc: Skipping cert validation enables MITM attacks.
rule.developer: Keep certificate validation enabled and implement proper trust chains.
rule.reviewer: Inspect all transport/client configs for bypass settings. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Gin SQL Query Concatenation Sink
rule.rule_desc: Detects dynamic SQL construction via string formatting.
rule.vuln_desc: Formatted SQL may introduce SQL injection vulnerabilities.
rule.developer: Use parameterized query APIs and strict input validation.
rule.reviewer: Trace user input into query format strings. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Gin Permissive CORS
rule.rule_desc: Detects wildcard CORS policies in Go services.
rule.vuln_desc: Wildcard origins can expose APIs to arbitrary websites.
rule.developer: Limit CORS to explicit trusted origins.
rule.reviewer: Validate CORS policy builders and middleware config. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Gin Hardcoded Secret Material
rule.rule_desc: Detects likely hardcoded secrets in Go source/config.
rule.vuln_desc: Hardcoded secrets can be extracted from code or binaries.
rule.developer: Use environment/config secret providers and rotate keys.
rule.reviewer: Confirm no production secrets are committed. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
